findings.object: Count of Customer Accounts Opted In LTD
findings.message: Measure 'Count of Customer Accounts Opted In LTD' uses FILTER(ALL(…)) anti-pattern. Use REMOVEFILTERS or direct predicate pushdown.
findings.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(…)) with REMOVEFILTERS or CALCULATE with predicates.

findings.object: Count of Customer Accounts Opted Out LTD
findings.message: Measure 'Count of Customer Accounts Opted Out LTD' uses FILTER(ALL(…)) anti-pattern. Use REMOVEFILTERS or direct predicate pushdown.
findings.fix: Replace FILTER(ALL(…)) with REMOVEFILTERS or CALCULATE with predicates.

findings.message: Measure 'Count of Customers Downgraded (As Today)' references columns without table qualifier: [Current Tier Number], [Previous Tier Number]. Fully qualify column references for clarity.

ruleResults.impactDescription: Forces full table scan in DirectQuery mode. Each FILTER(ALL) adds an unfiltered subquery to the generated SQL.
Readability and maintenance issue. No performance impact but increases risk of errors when columns are renamed.
Hidden/unused columns are still included in DirectQuery SQL. Increases read bytes and cost per query.
